title: imagebuildah.BuildDockerfiles(): create the jobs semaphore
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Make sure that, if we're given neither a concurrent jobs count nor a semaphore to use, we create a semaphore.  Otherwise, each platform in a multi-platform build will run with maximum concurrency, which isn't the desired default.  Our CLI always passes in a Jobs count, so it hasn't been affected by this problem.

#### How to verify it

The CLI always passes a number of jobs, so it doesn't trigger this.  It can be observed in programs that consume the library and set neither the `Jobs` nor the `JobSemaphore` field of a `define.BuildOptions`.  Podman, which consumes our CLI front-end, also won't stumble over it.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *nalind* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM. One doubt: @nalind We also had a default of of `jobs=1` if nothing is defined here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/imagebuildah/executor.go#L190 was that a no-op for users who were consuming library

This will now default to running the jobs based on the length of the stages.
LGTM
@nalind Answer @flouthoc question and then merge
/lgtm
/hold

That bit of logic is run by every `Executor` that we create - if we're building for N platforms, we run it N times.  If the caller previously didn't set either `Jobs` or `JobSemaphore`, `BuildDockerfiles()` wouldn't create a semaphore, so each `Executor` would end up creating its own, and they would run independently in parallel, so the observed behavior looked more like jobs=N than the intended jobs=1.

> This will now default to running the jobs based on the length of the stages.

Explicitly setting `Jobs` to 0 should have, and previously had, this effect.  We document 0 being treated as "as many as possible".  If I broke that, then this'll need to be revised before merging.
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
@nalind makes sense thanks for explanation. LGTM
